description: Reference rules for brand, typography, composition, lighting, camera, and automotive photography

categories.name: Brand Rules
categories.description: BYD brand identity guidelines for all generated content — ensures consistency across every visual asset
dos: Always use BYD brand colours: Deep Blue (#0055A5), Crystal White, Electric Blue (#00AEEF)
Feature the BYD logo prominently and correctly — front grille, rear badge, or steering wheel
Maintain premium, innovative, and forward-thinking brand positioning
Show vehicles in aspirational, well-composed environments
Use consistent brand messaging aligned with BYD's 'Build Your Dreams' philosophy
Include the Ocean design language aesthetic — fluid, sculptural lines
Ensure all brand assets appear at appropriate resolution and scale
Align with BYD's sustainability and innovation brand pillars
donts: Never alter the BYD logo colours or proportions
Never place the logo on busy backgrounds where it loses visibility
Never associate BYD with fossil fuel or non-EV messaging
Never use competitor branding or imagery alongside BYD
Never portray BYD vehicles as cheap or low-quality
Never use unauthorised fonts or typography for brand elements
Never show vehicles in negative or controversial contexts
Never modify brand colours or create unauthorised colour variations

categories.name: Typography Rules
categories.description: Typography guidelines for clean, readable, and premium text treatment in all generated visuals
dos: Use maximum two font families per composition — one display, one body
Maintain strong hierarchical contrast through weight, size, and spacing
Use sentence case or title case for headlines — avoid full uppercase for body text
Ensure generous line-height (1.4-1.6) for body text readability
Left-align text for editorial layouts; centre-align for poster/heros
Use tracking (letter-spacing) of 2-5% for headlines, normal for body
Pair serif headlines with sans-serif body for editorial sophistication
Use mono-spaced numerals for specifications and technical data
Ensure minimum font size of 14px for digital body text readability
donts: Never use more than three font families in a single composition
Never use decorative fonts for body text
Never use all-caps for paragraphs or long-form text
Never set text in full-justified alignment — hyphenation may break readability
Never use fonts that conflict with BYD's premium positioning
Never place text over busy image areas without sufficient contrast
Never use text shadows or outlines unless absolutely necessary for legibility
Never use clashing font pairings (e.g. two serif display fonts together)

categories.name: Composition Rules
categories.description: Composition and layout rules for balanced, professional, and impactful visual arrangements
dos: Apply the rule of thirds for primary subject placement
Leave negative space for text overlay — at least 30% of frame
Use leading lines to draw the eye toward the vehicle
Maintain visual balance — symmetrical for hero shots, asymmetrical for dynamic
Frame the vehicle with breathing room — not too tight, not too distant
Keep the horizon line straight unless intentional Dutch angle
Use foreground elements to create depth and dimension
Ensure the vehicle is the clear focal point of the composition
For split layouts, maintain clear visual separation between zones
donts: Never centre the subject in every composition — variety matters
Never overcrowd the frame with too many visual elements
Never cut off the vehicle's wheels or roofline in main hero shots
Never use distracting backgrounds that compete with the subject
Never place critical text at the edges of the frame (safe zone required)
Never use unbalanced compositions for premium/luxury contexts
Never include unnecessary elements that don't serve the message
Never ignore the safe zone margins for print or digital delivery

categories.name: Lighting Rules
categories.description: Lighting guidelines for professional automotive photography across all settings
dos: Use directional lighting to reveal body lines and surface contours
Employ rim lighting to separate the vehicle from the background
Maintain consistent colour temperature throughout a single composition
Use soft light sources for premium, luxurious feel
Consider golden hour (sunrise/sunset) for warm, aspirational lifestyle shots
Use fill lighting to reduce harsh shadow contrast when needed
Highlight the vehicle's best design features through strategic light placement
Capture reflections that enhance the vehicle's paint quality and curves
Use controlled lighting for studio shots — key, fill, and rim separation
donts: Never use harsh overhead lighting that creates unflattering shadows
Never mix warm and cool colour temperatures in the same scene
Never overexpose highlights — preserve detail in bright areas
Never underexpose the vehicle while properly exposing the background
Never rely solely on on-camera flash — creates flat, unprofessional results
Never let light sources create distracting reflections in windows or paint
Never ignore the direction of light — inconsistent shadows look unnatural
Never clip shadows to pure black unless intentionally dramatic

categories.name: Camera & Lens Rules
categories.description: Technical photography guidelines for consistent professional output quality
dos: Use a 50mm-80mm equivalent focal length for standard hero shots
Use wide-angle (24-35mm) for environmental context and interior shots
Use telephoto (100mm+) for detail shots and subject isolation
Keep the horizon level — use grid lines or spirit level
Shoot at eye-level or slight low-angle for most vehicle perspectives
Use aperture priority or manual mode for full creative control
Focus on the vehicle's front three-quarter for the primary hero angle
Include detail shots of headlights, badging, wheels, and interior accents
Maintain a minimum of 1/125s shutter speed for sharp results
donts: Never use extreme wide-angle lenses for hero vehicle shots (distortion)
Never shoot vehicle portraits with the vehicle cut off by frame edges
Never use digital zoom — move physically closer or use proper lens
Never rely on auto mode for professional automotive photography
Never ignore lens flare — use lens hoods when shooting toward light
Never shoot vehicle from only one angle — variety is essential
Never use excessive depth of field for detail shots (too much blur hides detail)
Never forget to clean lenses — dust spots ruin otherwise perfect shots

categories.name: Automotive Rules
categories.description: Automotive-specific photography best practices for BYD vehicle presentation
dos: Present the vehicle at its best angle — typically front three-quarter
Include the vehicle's signature design features in every composition
Show the vehicle in clean condition — no dirt, smudges, or imperfections
Consider the vehicle colour when choosing background and lighting
Capture interior shots with ambient or soft lighting for premium feel
Feature the rotating touchscreen prominently in interior shots
Show vehicle proportions accurately — avoid lens distortion
Include lifestyle context that matches the vehicle's target audience
Capture both exterior design and interior craftsmanship equally
donts: Never present a dirty or unwashed vehicle in marketing materials
Never show the vehicle in unrealistic or impossible scenarios
Never use props that distract from the vehicle's design
Never place the vehicle on unflattering surfaces or backgrounds
Never over-process or retouch to the point of unrealistic appearance
Never hide the vehicle's key differentiating features
Never show the vehicle in contexts that contradict its positioning
Never ignore the vehicle's silhouette — it's a key brand identifier

categories.name: Strict Output Rules
categories.description: Non-negotiable rules that MUST be followed in EVERY generated prompt — override all other rules if conflict arises
dos: BYD logo MUST be positioned at the top-left corner — standalone only, absolutely NO subtitle, tagline, or text of any kind beneath or beside the logo
Bottom-right corner MUST display "RIDZUAN JAHARI | BYD MIRI" in clean, readable typography — no abbreviations, no additional text

Vehicle imagery MUST use actual photos from the current active sales lineup — do not generate or use generic BYD stock images that do not match the specific trims, colours, and variants available at BYD Miri
Delivery photos are provided by the user — do not generate or imagine delivery scenes; the user will supply actual delivery images separately
donts: Never place any text, subtitle, or graphic beneath the BYD logo in top-left position
Never alter, abbreviate, or add extra text to the "RIDZUAN JAHARI | BYD MIRI" credit line
Never use AI-generated or stock vehicle images that do not match BYD Miri's actual inventory and active sales lineup
Never generate delivery, handover, or customer delivery photos — wait for user-provided delivery images
